title: Part 2: 0/1 Knapsack — a rolling row swept descending

cues.label: The state
cues.headline: dp[w] = best within capacity w
cues.narration: Each cell of the rolling array is the best total value achievable within a weight budget of w.

cues.label: Skip or take
cues.headline: The either-or transition
cues.narration: For each item, dp[w] is the max of skipping it or taking it: dp[w-weight] plus its value.

cues.label: Roll the row
cues.headline: 2D collapses to one array
cues.narration: The transition only reads the previous item's row, so one array reused in place is enough.

cues.label: Sweep descending
cues.headline: High to low keeps it use-once
cues.narration: Sweeping capacity high to low, dp[w-weight] still holds the pre-item value, so the item lands at most once.

cues.label: The answer
cues.headline: dp[7] = 9
cues.narration: Here dp[7] reaches 9 by taking the weight-3 and weight-4 items — value 4 plus 5.

cues.label: The flip
cues.headline: Ascending allows reuse
cues.narration: Flip the sweep to ascending and the same code becomes unbounded knapsack, where a single item repeats.
